description: Site context options with regulatory explanations for equipment qualification per FDA QSMR, 21 CFR 820, and ISO 13485.

regulatoryReferences.cleanroom: ISO 14644-1:2015; FDA Guidance for Aseptic Processing; 21 CFR 820.70
regulatoryReferences.productContact: 21 CFR 820.70; ISO 13485:2016 Clause 7.5; FDA QSR design controls

cleanroomClasses.label: ISO 1
cleanroomClasses.help: ~10 particles ≥0.1 µm/m³. Ultra-clean environment for semiconductor, nanotechnology, or critical pharmaceutical operations.

cleanroomClasses.label: ISO 2
cleanroomClasses.help: ~100 particles ≥0.1 µm/m³. Used for high-purity pharmaceutical or biotech processes.

cleanroomClasses.label: ISO 3
cleanroomClasses.help: ~1,000 particles ≥0.1 µm/m³. Very high cleanliness for critical manufacturing.

cleanroomClasses.label: ISO 5
cleanroomClasses.help: ~100,000 particles ≥0.1 µm/m³ (Class 100 equivalent). Required for aseptic filling and critical assembly per FDA and ISO 13485.
cleanroomClasses.typicalUse: Grade A zone, aseptic filling, critical product assembly

cleanroomClasses.label: ISO 6
cleanroomClasses.help: ~1,000,000 particles ≥0.5 µm/m³. High cleanliness for non-sterile but controlled processes.
cleanroomClasses.typicalUse: Grade C, buffer zones, some packaging

cleanroomClasses.label: ISO 7
cleanroomClasses.help: ~352,000 particles ≥0.5 µm/m³ (Class 10,000 equivalent). Common for medical device manufacturing and sterilization support per FDA guidance.
cleanroomClasses.typicalUse: Sterilization support, packaging, assembly, Grade D

cleanroomClasses.label: ISO 8
cleanroomClasses.help: ~3,520,000 particles ≥0.5 µm/m³ (Class 100,000 equivalent). General controlled environment.

cleanroomClasses.label: Not in a Cleanroom
cleanroomClasses.help: Equipment located in an uncontrolled or non-classified area (e.g., warehouse, general manufacturing floor).

productContact.label: Product contact
productContact.help: Product contact means the equipment or its surfaces (including processing aids, fixtures, or direct contact surfaces) come into direct or indirect contact with the finished medical device, its components, or in-process product during manufacturing. Per 21 CFR 820.70 and ISO 13485, product-contact equipment requires validated cleaning, materials qualification, and contamination control. Non-product-contact equipment may still require qualification for supporting processes.
productContact.regulatoryNote: 21 CFR 820.70(b) requires validation of processes that cannot be fully verified; product contact drives cleaning validation and material compatibility.

productionThroughput.label: Low
productionThroughput.help: Infrequent use; few runs per week or month. Risk-based qualification may emphasize initial validation with reduced revalidation frequency.

productionThroughput.label: Medium
productionThroughput.help: Regular use; multiple runs per week. Standard qualification depth and revalidation intervals apply per 21 CFR 820.75 and ISO 13485.

productionThroughput.label: High
productionThroughput.help: High-frequency or continuous use; daily or multi-shift operation. May require enhanced monitoring, SPC, and shorter revalidation intervals.
